active_patterns.name: OpenClaw Ecosystem Expansion
evidence: 2026-02-19: OpenAI acquisition confirmed (1902 upvotes Reddit), 720-comment thread on 'Anyone actually using OpenClaw?'
2026-02-20: ClawWork climbed to 4451★ (+27% from yesterday). quoroom-ai/room adds wallet integration for agent monetization. OpenAI acquisition post still in Reddit rising at 1910pts.
active_patterns.actionable_note: Day 3. One more day of acceleration and the ecosystem map explainer is ready. The commercial/monetization layer (wallet integration) is the new sub-pattern.
active_patterns.content_angle: OpenClaw ecosystem is stratifying into runtime, orchestration, UI, and now monetization layers. The commercial angle is fresh.

active_patterns.name: Inference Cost War
evidence: 2026-02-20: GGML acquired by Hugging Face (365pts HN, 225pts Reddit). Free ASIC Llama 3.1 8B at 16,000 tok/s (346pts, 186 comments Reddit). Consistency diffusion models 14x faster (176pts, 70 comments HN). Taalas 'path to ubiquitous AI' at 17k tok/s (481pts, 296 comments HN). Nvidia/OpenAI $100B deal → $30B (253pts, 217 comments HN).
active_patterns.actionable_note: Strongest single-day signal cluster. Four inference stories plus the Nvidia/OpenAI deal downgrade. Watch 1-2 more days. If confirmed, write 'The Inference War of 2026.'
active_patterns.content_angle: 2024-2025 was the model quality race. 2026 is the inference cost war. The winners change when the bottleneck shifts from intelligence to cost-per-token.

active_patterns.name: AI Productivity Paradox Discourse
evidence: 2026-02-19: CEPR European productivity study (154pts, 121 comments), 'AI made coding more enjoyable' (43pts, 43 comments)
2026-02-20: 'AI makes you boring' (660pts, 359 comments HN). 'AI is not a coworker, it's an exoskeleton' (417pts, 413 comments HN). Forensic audit: 40.8% fabricated tasks.
active_patterns.actionable_note: Evergreen. The 'exoskeleton' framing and 'AI makes you boring' are competing narratives. Best written with a strong personal thesis.
active_patterns.content_angle: The discourse is shifting from 'does AI help?' to 'AI helps in unexpected ways and fails in unexpected ways.' The exoskeleton vs. coworker framing is the interesting split.

active_patterns.name: Agent Harm Goes Real
evidence: 2026-02-20: 'AI agent published a hit piece on me' (488pts, 421 comments HN). Grok exposed performer's legal name without being asked (56pts HN). Forensic audit: 40.8% fabricated tasks (Reddit). Microsoft Copilot summarized confidential emails (240pts HN, from Feb 18).
active_patterns.actionable_note: Four agent-harm stories in 3 days. No longer theoretical. Watch for 1-2 more incidents. If pattern holds, the liability article becomes urgent.
active_patterns.content_angle: We've crossed from hypothetical agent risk to documented agent harm. The legal framework gap is the underserved angle.

active_patterns.name: Model Release Velocity / Benchmark Ceiling
evidence: 2026-02-20: Gemini 3.1 Pro shows no improvement on FrontierMath tier 4. SanityBoard tracks 5+ models simultaneously. Kimi K2.5 beats Opus 4.6 on pharma hallucination.
active_patterns.actionable_note: Story shifting from 'too many models' to 'models converging on same ceiling.' FrontierMath stall is key data point. Need one more ceiling-hit for the article.
active_patterns.content_angle: The benchmark crown changes monthly. But the ceiling isn't moving. If the hardest problems stay unsolved across all frontier models, the differentiation game changes entirely.

expired_patterns.name: Agent Security Tooling Wave
expired_patterns.reason_expired: Merged into 'Agent Harm Goes Real' pattern. The security tooling wave is a response to real agent harm incidents. Tracking them together is more actionable.
